issue.title: Doc: Add uninstall instructions for linux
issue.body:
Documentation update to add missing instructions for how to remove Joplin on linux.
For novice linux users it is not clear how to remove the app after installing.

comments.body: I have read the CLA Document and I hereby sign the CLA

I feel this whole topic of uninstallation on linux is closely mirroring community editable post [Unofficial alternative Joplin distributions - Lounge - Joplin Forum](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/unofficial-alternative-joplin-distributions/23703#p-81146-desktop-linux-2) Hence, I think it would make sense to split linux distro specific section to a separate post and expand it into installation/unistallation by distro. Here's a draft of such post -- AI generated (proton's lumo, scira, perplexity) and manually reviewed by me: content+styling for discourse but no physical testing.
Details

Below is a revised post suitable for the Joplin forum, using the referenced wiki for unofficial distributions and their maintainers.[1][2] This post groups Linux installation instructions by **installation method**, and for each unofficial distribution lists the maintainer nickname as in the “Unofficial alternative Joplin distributions” wiki. All non-AppImage methods are community-maintained and unsupported by the core project, so bugs and issues should be reported to their respective maintainers.[2][7][1] # How to install and cleanly uninstall Joplin on Linux (all methods) Always back up your profile (typically `~/.config/joplin-desktop` and any external sync/local data) before uninstalling or switching method.[12][13][2] *** ## 1. Official desktop script (AppImage) This is the officially supported desktop method on Linux and works on most distributions.[4][7] ### Install Run in a terminal: ```bash wget -O - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/laurent22/joplin/dev/Joplin_install_and_update.sh | bash ``` - Installs a Joplin AppImage under `~/.joplin/` for the current user.[1][12] - Creates a desktop entry and icon in your user’s XDG directories so it appears in your app menu.[12][1] ### Clean uninstall Close Joplin, then: ```bash # Remove AppImage + version file rm -f ~/.joplin/*.AppImage ~/.joplin/VERSION # Remove desktop entry and icon (names can slightly vary) rm -f ~/.local/share/applications/joplin.desktop \ ~/.local/share/applications/appimagekit-joplin.desktop rm -f ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/512x512/apps/joplin.png # Remove configuration and local profile (optional, this deletes local notes if not synced) rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop ``` If you created any additional custom launchers or icons, delete those as well. *** ## 2. Official terminal (CLI) via npm The CLI client is officially documented and can be installed with npm.[7] ### Install Example of a user-local install (recommended to avoid sudo with npm): ```bash # Choose a user-local prefix NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX="$HOME/.joplin-bin" npm install -g joplin --loglevel=error # Optionally add a global symlink (requires sudo) sudo ln -s "$HOME/.joplin-bin/bin/joplin" /usr/bin/joplin ``` Alternatively, if your system npm is already configured globally, you can use: ```bash sudo npm install -g joplin ``` ### Clean uninstall 1. Remove the npm package: ```bash npm uninstall -g joplin ``` 2. Remove any symlink you created: ```bash sudo rm -f /usr/bin/joplin ``` 3. Remove the chosen prefix directory if you used one: ```bash rm -rf ~/.joplin-bin ``` 4. Remove any CLI profile/config directories if different from the desktop profile on your system. *** ## 3. Flatpak (unofficial – maintainer: **catsout**) Flatpak builds are community-maintained and listed in the wiki as:[2][1] - Distribution: Flatpak - Name: Joplin - Maintainer: **catsout** ### Install First ensure Flatpak and Flathub are enabled, then: ```bash flatpak install flathub net.cozic.joplin_desktop ``` Run with: ```bash flatpak run net.cozic.joplin_desktop ``` ### Clean uninstall ```bash flatpak uninstall net.cozic.joplin_desktop ``` Optionally remove remaining Flatpak data for this app: ```bash rm -rf ~/.var/app/net.cozic.joplin_desktop ``` This removes the Flatpak sandbox configuration and data for Joplin. *** ## 4. Snap (unofficial – maintainer: **MrCarroll**) The wiki lists the Snap desktop package as:[1][2] - Distribution: Snap - Name: Joplin Desktop - Maintainer: **MrCarroll** ### Install Ensure `snapd` is available, then: ```bash sudo snap install joplin-desktop ``` Joplin can be launched from your menu or with: ```bash snap run joplin-desktop ``` ### Clean uninstall ```bash sudo snap remove joplin-desktop ``` User configuration under `~/.config/joplin-desktop` created by earlier non-snap installs is not touched by Snap and must be removed manually if you want a fully clean system. *** ## 5. Arch / AUR builds (unofficial – maintainers as per wiki) The wiki lists several AUR packages for the desktop:[2][1] - `joplin-desktop` – Maintainers: **masterkorp / alfredopalhares** - `joplin-appimage` – Maintainer: **fsociety3765** - `joplin-desktop-bin` – Maintainer: **thynkon** Pre‑compiled AUR builds may also be available in Chaotic‑AUR.[1][2] ### 5.1 AUR helper basics You need an AUR helper like `yay`, `paru` or `aura`. Once installed, the general pattern is:[2][1] ```bash yay -S joplin-desktop # or joplin-appimage / joplin-desktop-bin ``` ### 5.2 Clean uninstall for AUR packages Use the same helper to remove the package and its dependencies: ```bash yay -Rns joplin-desktop # or joplin-appimage / joplin-desktop-bin ``` Then optionally remove user data: ```bash rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop rm -rf ~/.joplin # if the AppImage variant created this ``` If you switched between multiple AUR flavors, check your menu entries and remove stale `.desktop` files under `~/.local/share/applications`. *** ## 6. RPM / COPR (unofficial – maintainer: **taw00**) The wiki lists an RPM distribution as:[1][2] - Distribution: RPM - Name: joplin - Maintainer: **taw00** This is typically provided via a COPR or similar repository for Fedora and related distributions.[2] ### Install After enabling the appropriate repository (see that project’s instructions), you normally run: ```bash sudo dnf install joplin # Fedora-like # or, if using another RPM-based distro: sudo zypper install joplin ``` ### Clean uninstall ```bash sudo dnf remove joplin # or sudo zypper remove joplin ``` Then optionally remove user data: ```bash rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop ``` *** ## 7. Nix / NixOS (unofficial – maintainer: **Hugo Reeves**) For the desktop on Linux, the wiki lists:[1][2] - Distribution: Nix - Name: joplin-desktop - Maintainer: **Hugo Reeves** ### Install If Nix is installed: ```bash # NixOS nix-env -iA nixos.joplin-desktop # Non-NixOS nix-env -iA nixpkgs.joplin-desktop ``` ### Clean uninstall ```bash nix-env -e joplin-desktop ``` Then, as with other methods, remove configuration if desired: ```bash rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop ``` If you have multiple Nix profiles or generations, also prune old generations so Nix garbage collection can reclaim space. *** ## 8. Manual AppImage or other manual installs (unofficial, user-maintained) Some users download the AppImage or other binaries manually and place them in a custom directory such as `~/Applications` or `/opt/joplin`.[14][12] ### Install For example: ```bash mkdir -p ~/Applications cd ~/Applications wget https://example.com/path/to/Joplin-x.y.z.AppImage # replace with actual release URL chmod +x Joplin-*.AppImage ``` You may also create a custom `~/.local/share/applications/joplin.desktop` pointing to that AppImage for menu integration. ### Clean uninstall Delete the binary and any custom desktop/icon files: ```bash rm -f ~/Applications/Joplin-*.AppImage rm -f ~/.local/share/applications/joplin.desktop rm -f ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/512x512/apps/joplin.png rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop ``` If you installed under `/opt` or another system directory, remove it with `sudo rm -rf /opt/joplin` (or equivalent) and any system-wide desktop entries. *** ## 9. General notes on “clean” uninstall Regardless of distribution or package type, a truly clean uninstall on Linux always has two parts:[13][12][2] 1. Remove the installed program - Using the appropriate package manager (`snap remove`, `flatpak uninstall`, `yay -Rns`, `dnf remove`, `nix-env -e`, etc.) or by deleting the AppImage/script install. 2. Remove all user-level configuration and data (optional but often desired) - Typically: - `rm -rf ~/.config/joplin-desktop` - `rm -rf ~/.joplin` or any other Joplin-specific directories you created If you plan to reinstall or you rely on sync only as a backup, make sure you export or sync your notes before deleting profile directories. [1](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/unofficial-alternative-joplin-distributions/23703) [2](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/alternative-distributions-wiki-list/23677) [3](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/why-only-appimage-for-linux/28604) [4](https://www.reddit.com/r/linuxbrasil/comments/1jlayvb/qual_apelidos_voc%C3%AAs_usam_pra_distro_linux/) [5](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/joplin-desktop-pre-built-packages-for-debian-ubuntu-archlinux/33042) [6](https://joplinapp.org/help/install/) [7](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/desktop-linux-package-management-why-don-t-we-build-for-each-distro/8123) [8](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/installing-in-ubuntu/32557) [9](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/joplin-arnatious/14487) [10](https://zap-hosting.com/guides/docs/dedicated-linux-joplin/) [11](https://github.com/laurent22/joplin/issues/3824) [12](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/how-to-completely-uninstall-joplin-from-linux-mint/8410) [13](https://www.reddit.com/r/linux4noobs/comments/ttoxd9/need_to_uninstall_joplin/)

If you want, I can post it by myself, I just thought it's better to be community editable -- which is unavailable for my [discourse profile](https://discourse.joplinapp.org/u/amil/) it

> Snap removes most data automatically, but you can delete remaining app-specific dirs if present (path may vary by system):
> sudo rm -rf /var/snap/joplin-desktop
> User configuration under ~/.config/joplin-desktop created by earlier non-snap installs is not touched by Snap and must be removed manually if you want a fully clean system.

Just thought I'd correct the AI on these, the only thing that needs to be done is `snap remove joplin-desktop` and optionally with the `--purge` flag that I do not recommend people use. Removing the snap automatically removes user data but keeps a backup in snaps native tooling for 30 days incase that's unexpected behaviour for people allowing them to recover data; which is what `--purge` avoids, deleting everything instantly instead.

It doesn't use `~/.config/joplin-desktop` so that'd be deleting other packages data. Similarly there's nothing in `/var` to delete that isn't handled by the remove command but working on `/var/snapd` by hand can lead to issues with internal state, so it truly does just boil down to the one command or GUI equivalent.

> > Snap removes most data automatically, but you can delete remaining app-specific dirs if present (path may vary by system):
> > sudo rm -rf /var/snap/joplin-desktop
> > User configuration under ~/.config/joplin-desktop created by earlier non-snap installs is not touched by Snap and must be removed manually if you want a fully clean system.
> 
> Just thought I'd correct the AI on these, 

Thank you very much for your correction -- I've now deleted the paragraph. Additionally, I went ahead and published the revised version to the forum hoping to get corrections from other community maintainers as well.

https://discourse.joplinapp.org/t/how-to-install-and-cleanly-uninstall-joplin-on-linux-all-methods/48300?u=amil

Guess I'll speedrun discourse membership trust level to turn the post into a publicly editable wiki :sweat_smile:

Thank you for this PR but I feel this is not something that can be merged as it is, or fixed in an obvious way. As mentioned in the discussion above, the installation method should provide a way to uninstall the app, or provide documentation for it. We cannot reliably provide a general doc that will work in all cases.
